An EF-0 tornado touched down along Adger Road and moved across the south side of Easley. The tornado crossed several residential neighborhoods where numerous large limbs were snapped and several pine trees were uprooted. The tornado dissipated over a neighborhood near Crestview Road.

A very brief and weak EF-0 tornado touched down just west of Killian Farm Road. The tornado snapped a tree along with multiple large limbs. The tornado continued southeast crossing Caswell Road where additional limbs were snapped. The tornado dissipated in a wooded area long Highway 73 just west of the Catawba River.

A brief and weak EF-0 tornado touched down along Old Calhoun Falls Road near Mud Creek Road. The tornado moved northeast, paralleling Old Calhoun Falls Road, before dissipating near the intersection with Latimer Road. The tornado snapped multiple large limbs and uprooted a couple trees. Maximum estimated winds were 65 mph with a width of 20 yards.

A brief, weak, EF-0 tornado touched down just east of Percival Road, then traveled southeast. It dissipated over a wooded area away from any marked roads. Along this path, several softwood trees were snapped, and there was minor damage to a barn and playground.

A stream gauge on Edwards Branch at Sheffield Dr exceeded flood stage at 830 PM and returned to its banks around 920 PM.

The stream gauge on McMullen Creek above Lincrest Place exceeded flood stage at around 830 PM. Its still rising and approaching major flood stage as of 930 PM.
A stream gauge on McMullen Creek at Sharon View Rd exceeded flood stage at 840 PM. The stream was beginning to recede at this location as of 940 PM.
The gauge on Little Sugar Creek at Archdale Dr exceeded flood stage at 850 PM. As of 930 PM, the stream is still rising and near moderate flood stage.
A stream gauge on Little Sugar Creek at Hillside Ave exceeded flood stage at 855 PM. The stream was beginning to recede at this location as of 940 PM.

EM reported a small flash flood along Thomas Valley Road, tributaries draining into the Tuckasegee River rose rapidly. An unnamed creek washed out a bridge and a portion of the railroad. Another small creek, Bumgarner Branch, overflowed its banks, rerouting through a yard over 5 feet deep, requiring a water rescue from a home. No damage to campground along the river.

Deep street flooding in downtown Marion led to water damage in one building on Henderson St and another on Court St. Rapid rises occurred on Youngs Creek and led to a bridge being overtopped on Shop Dr.
911 caller requested water rescue on Owl Hollow Rd due to flooding on Jacktown Creek.
Three people rescued from floodwaters. Approximately 25 people evacuated from a campground off Deacon Drive. Three buildings and numerous RVs and vehicles flooded.

York County Emergency Management reported flooding of one lane of Bookout Rd, likely due to flooding of SF Fishing Creek.

York County EMA reported Phil Grant Rd flooded and washed out near Strait Rd, likely due to adjacent small drainage.

York County Emergency Management reported extensive residential yard flooding in the Gallant Meadows subdivision due to Big Dutchman Creek overflowing its banks. At least one shed off of Drawbridge Ct was inundated by shallow floodwaters. No roadways were reported to have flooded. Big Dutchman Creek crested at 11.45 feet at 130 PM. Minor Flood Stage is 11 feet. At the time of the report, Big Dutchman Creek was at 10.56 feet and falling.
